title: Transaction kind catalogue
description: The 12 transaction kinds the module supports. Each kind documents its trigger (which domain event fires it), typical amount semantics, canonical debit/credit ledger entry pair, and whether it participates in reversal chains. The TransactionRecorder consults this catalogue when building ledger entries; the SDUI kind-picker widget reads it to render tenant-facing options.

kinds.description: Money moved IN from a paid invoice. Fires when invoice.paid domain event fires. The primary revenue-recording path.

expected_ledger_entries.description: Money into the tenant's cash account

kinds.description: Money moved OUT via a refund. Fires when refund.issued domain event fires. ALWAYS paired with an adjustment transaction for revenue reversal (two transactions per refund).
kinds.pair_note: The paired adjustment transaction records [debit: refunds_expense, credit: revenue] — the revenue is un-recognized alongside the refund.

kinds.description: Money moved OUT via a chargeback. Fires when chargeback.filed domain event fires. Distinct from refund_issued — chargebacks are INVOLUNTARY (bank-initiated).

expected_ledger_entries.description: Money removed from cash by the bank

kinds.description: Discount recognized when a coupon is redeemed. Reduces revenue + recognizes a marketing/coupons expense.

expected_ledger_entries.description: Coupon discount recognized as marketing expense
Revenue reduction (offsetting the invoice-payment credit that will follow)

kinds.note: Coupon credits do NOT participate in reversal chains — a coupon can't be un-redeemed. If a coupon-redemption is voided, the associated invoice is refunded (fires refund_issued + adjustment transactions).

kinds.description: Tenant filed + paid tax to a regulator. Clears the tax_payable liability.

expected_ledger_entries.description: Money out of cash to the tax authority

kinds.note: Tax remittances are terminal — once paid to the regulator, they don't reverse. Errors in tax remittance handled via accounting_adjustment reversal manually authored by finance.

kinds.description: Payment gateway fee (Stripe / Paddle) charged. Recorded as a separate transaction from the associated invoice_payment for clean fee-tracking.

expected_ledger_entries.description: Money removed from cash by the gateway

kinds.note: Fee refunds (rare — some gateways reverse fees when a payment is fully refunded) recorded as accounting_adjustment reversals of the original fee_charge.

kinds.description: Manual accounting adjustment or paired reversal companion. Used for revenue reversal alongside refund_issued transactions + for manual admin ledger corrections.
kinds.trigger: internal — TransactionRecorder::recordAdjustment() OR POST /transactions/{id}/reverse with reversal_reason='accounting_adjustment'
kinds.expected_ledger_entries_note: Variable — the entries depend on the specific adjustment. For refund pairing: [debit: refunds_expense, credit: revenue]. For manual corrections: caller-specified.

kinds.description: Uncollectible invoice written off. Removes the receivable + recognizes a loss.
kinds.note: Distinct from refund_issued — a refund reverses a PAID transaction; a write-off recognizes a NEVER-PAID invoice as loss.

kinds.description: Cash moved from operational cash into a bank deposit account. External banking flow — the transaction records the movement; the actual bank integration happens outside this module.
Cash moved from bank deposit back to operational cash. External banking flow.
Explicit FX conversion — moving cash from one currency to another. Rare — most cross-currency handled implicitly via the transaction.currency + exchange_rate_to_base fields.
kinds.note: This is the ONE case where a transaction may have ledger entries in different currencies — two cash entries in different account_currency values. TransactionObserver.finalize() balances by base_currency-normalized amounts.

kinds.description: One-shot per-tenant transaction created at tenant provisioning. Establishes the ledger's start-of-history entry with all-zero balances (or non-zero when migrating from a legacy system).
kinds.expected_ledger_entries_note: Variable — for a fresh tenant, one zero entry per enabled account (net effect: zero). For legacy-migration tenants, non-zero starting balances derived from the legacy system.
kinds.note: Fires EXACTLY ONCE per tenant. Refuses to fire again — the observer refuses opening_balance kind when the tenant already has one.

notes: The kind enum is the authoritative list. Adding a new kind = updating this catalogue + updating the TransactionKind enum + updating the ChartOfAccountsRegistry if new accounts are needed + updating data/sample-double-entry-patterns.json.

The expected_ledger_entries per kind are the CANONICAL pattern. TransactionRecorder methods build these by default; the observer validates every entry matches an expected pattern for the kind (defense-in-depth against a caller passing wrong accounts).

participates_in_reversal=true means transactions of this kind can be reversed via TransactionReverser. participates_in_reversal=false means they're terminal — either they never reverse (opening_balance, coupon_credit, tax_remittance) or reversal is handled indirectly (fee_charge reversals happen via the invoice-refund flow).
